topic: Evaluating and Analysing Performance for Improvement (EAPI)
subject: pe

items.q: What is the first step in EAPI?
items.why: Students jump to solutions; analysis must begin by identifying what is good and weak.

items.q: A weakness caused by losing focus under pressure is mainly:
items.why: Students label every weakness as technical; causes can be technical, tactical, physical or psychological.

items.q: Why compare a performer to a 'model performer'?
items.correct: To pinpoint differences and areas to improve
items.why: Students misunderstand the purpose; the model performer is a benchmark for identifying improvement areas.

items.q: Comparing a performer to a 'model performer' helps to:
items.correct: Pinpoint differences and areas to improve
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Pinpoint differences and areas to improve. “Decide ticket prices” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: What is the purpose of an action plan in EAPI?
items.correct: To set out structured, progressive steps for improvement
items.why: Correct answer: To set out structured, progressive steps for improvement. “To record match scores only” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
